prompt: SELECT total_revenue AS revenue FROM ( SELECT SUM(amount) AS total_revenue FROM orders WHERE year = 2025 ) AS subquery;
EXPLAIN JOIN customers c ON c.customer_id = o.customer_id INNER JOIN orders o ON o.customer_id = o.customer_id;
HOW MANY UNIQUE active_users PER WEEK FROM the events table?
SELECT count(DISTINCT event_name) ,  SUM(event_amount) AS total_event_amount FROM events GROUP BY DAY(event_date);
SELECT product_id ,  SUM(product_sales) FROM sales WHERE year = 2025 GROUP BY product_id;
EXPLAIN CTE:
WITH monthly_revenue AS (
SELECT DATE_TRUNC('month', order_date) AS month, SUM(amount) AS revenue
FROM orders
GROUP BY month
SELECT * FROM monthly_revenue
SELECT total_product_return_rate ,  SUM(product_sales) FROM sales WHERE year = 2025 GROUP BY total_product_return_rate;
HOW MANY active_users PER DAY FROM the events table?
SELECT customers.customer_name ,  COUNT(DISTINCT orders.order_id) AS num_orders
FROM customers
JOIN orders ON customers.customer_id = orders.customer_id
GROUP BY customers.customer_name
SELECT product_category ,  SUM(product_sales) AS total_sales_amount FROM sales WHERE year = 2025 GROUP BY product_category;
HOW MANY active_users PER WEEKEND FROM the events table?
EXPLAIN SELECT c.customer_id ,  MAX(o.order_date) AS last_order FROM customers c
JOIN orders o ON c.customer_id = o.customer_id
GROUP BY c.customer_id;
SELECT total_events_per_user ,  SUM(event_amount) FROM events JOIN customers ON events.user_id = customers.customer_id GROUP BY users.customer_id;
HOW MANY active_users WITH more_than_10_orders FROM the events table?
EXPLAIN SELECT AVG(total_product_return_rate) AS avg_product_return_rate
SELECT product_category ,  SUM(product_sales) / SUM(total_product_sales) AS total_product_return_rate
FROM sales
GROUP BY product_category
) AS subquery;
SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT customer_id) ,  SUM(num_orders) AS total_num_orders
SELECT customers.customer_id ,  COUNT(DISTINCT orders.order_id) AS num_orders
GROUP BY customers.customer_id
SELECT product_category ,  SUM(product_sales_amount) FROM sales GROUP BY product_category HAVING SUM(product_sales_amount) > 100000;
SELECT events.user_id ,  COUNT(DISTINCT orders.order_id) FROM events JOIN orders ON events.user_id = orders.customer_id
GROUP BY user_id ORDER BY COUNT(DISTINCT orders.order_id) DESC LIMIT 1;
HOW MANY active_users WITH less_than_5_orders FROM the events table?
EXPLAIN SELECT AVG(active_users_per_week) AS avg_active_users_per_week
SELECT WEEK(event_date) ,  COUNT(DISTINCT active_users) / WEEK(COUNT(*)) AS active_users_per_week
FROM events
GROUP BY WEEK(event_date)
SELECT total_monthly_revenue ,  SUM(monthly_revenue) AS total_monthly_sales
SELECT DATE_TRUNC('month', order_date) AS month, SUM(amount) AS monthly_revenue
EXPLAIN JOIN customers c ON c.customer_id = o.customer_id INNER JOIN orders o ON o.customer_id = c.customer_id WHERE o.order_date >= '2025-01-01';
SELECT product_category ,  AVG(product_sales_amount) AS avg_product_sales_amount FROM sales WHERE year = 2024 GROUP BY product_category;

SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT user_id) FROM orders WHERE order_date BETWEEN '2020-01-01' AND '2020-12-31'
